target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics at descriptor registration
target.body:
> **AI-assisted PR.** Implemented and verified with Claude (Claude Code). I have reviewed and understand the change and own the final diff.

## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups silently hid tools from the plan with no observable signal. Plugin authors had no way to know their descriptor was malformed.
- Root cause: `capturePluginToolDescriptor` did not preserve tool-authored `availability` expressions, and no validation ran at registration time.
- Fix: preserve `availability` on captured descriptors, validate group shapes recursively at the descriptor-capture boundary (including nested `allOf`/`anyOf` groups), and surface `unsupported-signal` diagnostics via `console.warn` once at registration time.

Fixes #92361

## Change

`src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts` — +84/-9
`src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts` — +198

Key changes:

1. **Preserve plugin-authored `availability`** on captured tool descriptors (previously dropped)
2. **Recursive shape guard** `hasValidAvailabilityGroupShape`: validates every `allOf`/`anyOf` group at any nesting level is an array, with non-object entry defense (plugin data is arbitrary JS — null, primitives, and arrays are skipped)
3. **Registration-time diagnostics**: evaluate the preserved availability once at capture time, surface `unsupported-signal` warnings immediately

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**:
Malformed tool descriptors with empty availability groups now produce a visible `console.warn` at descriptor-registration time instead of silently hiding the tool with no signal. This includes nested malformed expressions — a non-array `anyOf` inside an `allOf` is caught at the boundary.

**Real environment tested**:
Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw PR branch @ db7b0418, tsx for runtime probing.

**Before-fix evidence**:
```text
# Empty anyOf → tool silently hidden, no warning, no log
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { capturePluginToolDescriptor } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.js';
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'proof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});
console.log('No warning was emitted — tool silently captured with broken availability');
"
# Output: No warning was emitted — tool silently captured with broken availability
```

The tool descriptor is captured and later hidden by `buildToolPlan` with zero observable signal. Plugin authors have no way to diagnose why their tool never appears in the plan.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { capturePluginToolDescriptor } from './src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.js';

// Test 1: empty anyOf (top-level)
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'proof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 2: empty allOf (top-level)
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'allof_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { allOf: [] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 3: nested non-array anyOf inside allOf
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'nested_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { allOf: [{ anyOf: 'not-array' }] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 4: non-object entries inside group — must not crash
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'mixed_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { allOf: [{ kind: 'always' }, null, 'str', 42] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});

// Test 5: valid nested expression (no warning expected)
capturePluginToolDescriptor({
  pluginId: 'proof-plugin',
  tool: { name: 'valid_tool', description: 'test', parameters: { type: 'object' }, availability: { anyOf: [{ kind: 'always' }, { allOf: [{ kind: 'always' }] }] } } as any,
  optional: false,
});
"
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```text
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/allof_tool): Empty availability allOf group
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/nested_tool): Non-array availability group — allOf/anyOf values must be arrays
```

- Empty `anyOf` → `console.warn` emitted ✅
- Empty `allOf` → `console.warn` emitted ✅
- Nested non-array `anyOf` inside `allOf` → caught and warned ✅
- Non-object entries (`null`, string, number) inside group array → no crash ✅
- Valid nested expression → no warning ✅

**Observed result after the fix**:
Descriptor authors immediately see which tool has a malformed availability expression at registration time, including nested malformed groups. Non-object entries (null, primitives, arrays) inside group arrays pass the shape guard safely and are left for the evaluator to diagnose. The evaluator and planner stay pure.

**What was not tested**:
Live gateway startup with a real external plugin that has malformed availability metadata. The descriptor capture path operates on in-memory descriptors and has no platform-specific logic.

## Tests and validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       12 passed (12)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       67 passed (67)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests       6 passed (6)

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
# passed
```

New test cases:
1. warns when tool has empty availability `anyOf` group
2. warns when tool has empty availability `allOf` group
3. warns when `allOf` value is not an array
4. warns when `anyOf` value is not an array
5. does not warn for valid nested availability groups
6. warns for nested malformed availability groups
7. does not crash on non-object entries inside availability groups
8. does not warn for valid availability expression (kind-based)
9. does not warn when tool has no availability
10. does not warn for non-expression availability metadata
11. preserves availability on the captured descriptor

## Risk / scope

- User-visible behavior change: No — descriptors are a developer contract, not user-facing runtime behavior
- Config/environment/migration: No
- Security/auth/network: No
- Highest risk: `console.warn` fires once per captured descriptor at registration time. Descriptors are cached after first capture, so the warning fires exactly once per unique tool per cache lifetime.
- Mitigation: `unsupported-signal` only fires for malformed descriptors (empty allOf/anyOf, non-array group values). Runtime conditions like auth-missing are explicitly excluded. Non-object entries in group arrays are safely skipped by the shape guard.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` / `evaluateExpression` — still pure, no side effects
- `buildToolPlan` — still a deterministic planner, no callbacks
- `AnyAgentTool` type — no new contract field; reading through narrowed record (same pattern as existing `label` read)
- Plugin SDK exports — no public API surface change

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
